signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + Retail Extreme
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,437 (specs fading hawkish bias) vs RBNZ hike probability priced for 27 May. Retail sentiment at 87% long (extreme) diverges from institutional short positioning. If RBNZ delivers a hike or hawkish guidance, the crowded spec short and retail long positioning creates a high-velocity volatility window. NZD/USD at 0.5857.

signals.title: USD/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: USD/AUD spread at 1.039% confirms a dominant G8 bilateral theme as USD outperforms (+0.386%) and AUD lags (-0.653%). Escalating Iran tensions and oil price rallies support USD safe-haven demand while weighing on risk-sensitive AUD. Despite a -60bp carry disadvantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBA 4.35%), USD strength persists on geopolitical escalation. AUD/USD at 0.7154.
signals.watch_level: Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun to shift the carry narrative.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Options Skew + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.91 testing the 160.00 psychological barrier where BoJ intervention risk rises. 25d RR at -1.73 (strong put skew) signals the options market is paying a significant premium for JPY strength/USD downside protection despite the +283bp SOFR-TONA carry advantage. JPY COT LF net short -70,605 remains crowded, increasing reversal magnitude if the 160.00 level triggers a policy response.
signals.watch_level: 160.00 remains the critical level for BoJ verbal or physical intervention.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Elevated Vol Premium + Supply Shocks
signals.text: GBP/USD IV at 11.0% vs HV30 at 7.2% generates a +3.8% Vol Risk Premium (VRP), signaling elevated hedging demand. BoE's Greene highlights that the bank should not look through negative supply shocks (US-Iran war), maintaining a hawkish floor. GBP/USD at 1.3357 remains supported by a - (SONIA 3.73% vs SOFR 3.56%) despite technical pressure below the 200-DMA.
signals.watch_level: Next BoE meeting on 18 Jun determines if the hawkish floor holds.
